encounters.hpi: Patient is in their usual state of health. Routine follow-up visit.
encounters.assessment: Routine. No acute issues.
encounters.plan: Continue current regimen. Return PRN.

encounters.hpi: 74-year-old woman with 3-day history of acute gastroenteritis: watery diarrhea, abdominal cramping, fever to 102. Recent church potluck attendance with potato salad. Several other attendees also ill.
encounters.assessment: Confirmed Salmonellosis. ICD-10 A02.9. SCT 302229004.
encounters.plan: Supportive care, oral rehydration. Stool culture confirmed. Antibiotics not indicated unless severe.

encounters.hpi: 10-year-old woman from Phoenix presenting with progressive cough x 4 weeks, low-grade fever, fatigue, night sweats. Reports significant exposure to wind-blown soil dust during recent yard work. No skin rash. Lives in cocci-endemic region.
encounters.assessment: Confirmed Coccidioidomycosis (Valley Fever). ICD-10 B38.9. SCT 5294002.
encounters.plan: Fluconazole 400mg daily x 3-6 months. Repeat serology in 4 weeks. ID consult if disseminated.

encounters.hpi: 55-year-old man with chronic cough x 6 weeks, weight loss 12 lb, night sweats, low-grade fever. Recent immigration from endemic region. Household contacts identified.
encounters.assessment: Confirmed Tuberculosis, active disease. ICD-10 A15.9. SCT 56717001.
encounters.plan: RIPE therapy initiated. Respiratory isolation. Contact tracing through ADHS TB program.

encounters.hpi: 32-year-old woman with fever, headache, myalgias x 5 days. Maculopapular rash on wrists and ankles, now spreading to trunk. Recent hiking in eastern AZ. Tick exposure reported.
encounters.assessment: Confirmed Spotted fever rickettsiosis (e.g., Rocky Mountain spotted fever). ICD-10 A77.0. SCT 186772009.
encounters.plan: Doxycycline 100mg BID x 7-14 days. Continue until afebrile x 3 days.

active_problems.name: Spotted fever rickettsiosis (e.g., Rocky Mountain spotted fever)

encounters.hpi: 83-year-old woman with fever, cough, coryza, conjunctivitis x 3 days, now with maculopapular rash starting at hairline. Unvaccinated. Recent international travel.
encounters.assessment: Confirmed Measles (rubeola). ICD-10 B05.9. SCT 14189004.
encounters.plan: Supportive care, vitamin A. Airborne isolation. Contact tracing critical.

encounters.hpi: 16-year-old man from Tuba City presenting with progressive cough x 2 weeks, low-grade fever, fatigue, night sweats. Reports significant exposure to wind-blown soil dust during recent yard work. No skin rash. Lives in cocci-endemic region.
encounters.assessment: Confirmed Coccidioidomycosis (Valley Fever). ICD-10 B38.9. SCT 5294002.
encounters.plan: Fluconazole 400mg daily x 3-6 months. Repeat serology in 4 weeks. ID consult if disseminated.

encounters.hpi: 22-year-old man with fever, headache, myalgias x 5 days. Maculopapular rash on wrists and ankles, now spreading to trunk. Recent hiking in eastern AZ. Tick exposure reported.

encounters.plan: Doxycycline 100mg BID x 7-14 days. Continue until afebrile x 3 days.

encounters.hpi: 11-year-old man with confirmed Rabies in a human.
encounters.assessment: Confirmed Rabies in a human. ICD-10 A82.9. SCT 14168008.
encounters.plan: Per protocol.

encounters.hpi: 61-year-old woman with 3-day history of acute gastroenteritis: watery diarrhea, abdominal cramping, fever to 102. Recent church potluck attendance with potato salad. Several other attendees also ill.
encounters.assessment: Confirmed Salmonellosis. ICD-10 A02.9. SCT 302229004.
encounters.plan: Supportive care, oral rehydration. Stool culture confirmed. Antibiotics not indicated unless severe.

encounters.hpi: 12-year-old man with painless genital ulcer noted 3 weeks ago, now resolved. Now with diffuse maculopapular rash including palms and soles. RPR ordered.
encounters.assessment: Confirmed Syphilis. ICD-10 A53.9. SCT 76272004.
encounters.plan: Benzathine penicillin G 2.4M units IM. Confirm with VDRL and FTA-ABS. Partner notification.

encounters.hpi: 84-year-old woman with fever, severe headache, hepatitis x 2 weeks. Goat farmer. Recent kidding season with placenta exposure.
encounters.assessment: Confirmed Q fever. ICD-10 A78. SCT 16241000.
encounters.plan: Doxycycline 100mg BID x 14 days. Echocardiogram to evaluate for endocarditis.

encounters.hpi: 50-year-old man from Tuba City presenting with progressive cough x 2 weeks, low-grade fever, fatigue, night sweats. Reports significant exposure to wind-blown soil dust during recent yard work. No skin rash. Lives in cocci-endemic region.
encounters.assessment: Confirmed Coccidioidomycosis (Valley Fever). ICD-10 B38.9. SCT 5294002.
encounters.plan: Fluconazole 400mg daily x 3-6 months. Repeat serology in 4 weeks. ID consult if disseminated.

encounters.plan: Benzathine penicillin G 2.4M units IM. Confirm with VDRL and FTA-ABS. Partner notification.

encounters.plan: Doxycycline 100mg BID + rifampin 600mg daily x 6 weeks.

encounters.hpi: 26-year-old woman with dysuria and purulent urethral discharge x 5 days. Multiple recent sexual partners. Requesting STI screen.
encounters.assessment: Confirmed Gonorrhea. ICD-10 A54.9. SCT 15628003.
encounters.plan: Ceftriaxone 500mg IM single dose. Test for chlamydia coinfection. Partner notification.

encounters.hpi: 62-year-old man with sudden fever, painful inguinal lymphadenopathy. Reports flea bites and dead prairie dogs on property in Coconino County. Suspected bubonic plague.
encounters.assessment: Confirmed Plague. ICD-10 A20.9. SCT 58750007.
encounters.plan: Streptomycin 1g IM BID x 10 days OR doxycycline. Hospital isolation.

encounters.hpi: 81-year-old woman with intermittent fever, night sweats, arthralgia x 4 weeks. Cattle rancher in Cochise County. Reports consumption of raw milk from own herd.

encounters.hpi: 88-year-old woman with abrupt fever, myalgias, then progressive dyspnea over 48 hours. Recent rodent exposure cleaning out cabin in northern AZ. Now hypoxic, requires admission.
encounters.assessment: Confirmed Hantavirus infection. ICD-10 B33.4. SCT 63702000.
encounters.plan: ICU admission. Supportive care, mechanical ventilation as needed. Ribavirin not effective.
